storyInfo: Kestrel and her student escort a merchant to the border, suffering a bandit raid along the way. For the price of her blood, Kestrel teaches her student what a 'bite mark' means, but unwittingly gives rise to a whole new misunderstanding...

attributes.content: In the dry season, the sun's ruthless heat beats down on the great land. A lifeless landscape stretches to infinity. All is a singular parched yellow.
But even in these conditions, people still traverse through these barrens in order to survive. A burdenbeast wagon makes its way through the rocky sand.
Kestrel sits at its front, looking ahead at the empty wastes. The only obstacle they've faced so far is the intense heat. Just the last leg of the trip left, and this escort mission is over.
Telara, how's the progress?
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: You spent all yesterday studying back there, and only came out for a few quick bites of lunch before going back. You got a feel for it now, yeah?
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: She turns to the girl sitting in the front with her. Telara. She's clutching a copybook, and pauses before shaking her head at Kestrel.
I'm still pretty lost, Kestrel... Understanding these designs is really hard...
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: Like these ones here, I can't even tell the difference between them. I never stop mixing up their meanings.
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: I feel like I'm a long way away from drawing out a full 'bite mark'...
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: Well... doesn't have to be that complicated.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: 'Bite marks' originated as something to remember battles by. They're our way of recording the past. What's that word those people like to use?
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Uh... heritage?
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: Yeah, pretty much that.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: As long as you know how the tribe fights, and how they see glory, then the marks basically design themselves.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: I'm not asking you to be like those culture nuts and memorize what every single design means. What matters is that you have that feel.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: I know, you're right, and the idea makes sense to me. But I haven't fought in any battles like you. I don't have that experience.
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: I'm just a kid who grew up in a sleepy little town. Earlier it was my first time even seeing armed guards.
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: So how am I supposed to get a feel for it...?
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: See, that's why I brought you out on this escort mission. Otherwise, I'd have left you with the branch office people.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: How do I explain this to you... 'bite marks' are also sort of a label that stands for a warrior...
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: But you've never been in battle, right, so how do I explain the mindset of a warrior to you...
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Oh wait, Kestrel, you've painted new marks since leaving your tribe, right?
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: Which means you drew up all those patterns from scratch. What was your mindset with those?
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: Well, I did do some new marks at Rhodes Island, but...
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: I never stopped being a warrior there. My life's never changed like that. Those designs are inseparable from the battle ones...
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: I just took the old ways and designed new marks with them.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: I'm going off the way you taught me, though! Why do the designs I draw end up so wonky?
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: Maybe I'm just too dumb for this...
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: Kestrel can't help but laugh when she hears that. She reaches over to tousle Telara's hair.
Not letting you call yourself that!
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: And even if you don't have the talent for it, I'll still keep teaching you till you've got it.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: It can be hard to get a handle on what 'bite marks' mean, but you've got this, kid, trust me. I believe in you.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Doesn't matter if you can't fight. I'll take you out to see all the legends of these barrens, and then one day you'll be able to paint a true 'bite mark' of your own, I guarantee it.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: With that, Kestrel returns to watching ahead. Her vision fills with a vacant wilderness under the harsh sun. It still feels the same all these years later.
Free of Kestrel's tousling, Telara shakes her hair back into shape. Her gaze fixes on the tutor she met not so long ago.
Kestrel, can I say something? You're nothing like most warriors.
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: Where's this coming from?
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Well—
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: Hey, Miss Kestrel! How are things up front?
Before Telara can finish, a Kuranta man pokes his head out from inside the wagon.
Days of relentless travel have worn into the merchant's suntanned face. The wide, toothy grin he stretches his mouth into feels oddly unnatural.
Relax, it's all clear. We'll reach the border market real soon, and after we pass through it, we're basically out of Sargon.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: From there, you're free to keep going north with your stuff. Won't need any clearance.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Just like I told you when we set out, right? It just looks bleak—it's not all that risky. Plenty of merchant caravans pick this route.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Still, it doesn't hurt to be wary. I won't complain if nothing happens, obviously, but on the off-chance something does...
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: I've only done business a few times in Sargon. Every time I bring my stock out here and it's uninhabited barrens for days on end, I get a little antsy.
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: I'm a stranger in a strange land. Hiring you Rhodes Island folk is sound insurance to me.
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: Haha! Didn't peg you for the cautious type. Still, no worries. If anything happens, I guarantee you won't suffer a scratch.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Prioritize my goods, not my life. It's not easy for them to make the trip.
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: Not your life... what?
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Look, whatever, I'm not digging into your mind. Just sit inside and get some rest, and I'll wake you up when we're at the border market.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: That not-quite-natural smile plays across his face again, and he nods and retreats back inside the wagon.
Alright, I hear you. I'll just sit in here and be patient...
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: The merchant's speech is buried under the rattle of the burdenbeast wagon's wheels. Kestrel and Telara share a brief look, then break into their own grins.
Go sit in the back with him, Telara. The sun'll be at its worst soon.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: You'll figure out your 'bite marks'. But sitting out here getting baked isn't gonna help, and I can watch the road just fine on my own.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Once we're at the border market, we can get back to working on it. Sound good?
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Yup!
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: Move it!
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: The oasis where the border market lies comes into view on the distant horizon, exactly when a horn sounds the charge.
The burdenbeasts come to a quick halt at the sound, and Kestrel's hand flies to the hilt of her sword. In closes the din of galloping through sand, fast encroaching.
The merchant peers out in agitation, the sweat on his face cold against the heat of the desert.
Miss Kestrel, this... this isn't normal, right? Did we run into...?
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: Why are we stopped? Can't we shake them?
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: No. Not unless you're willing to dump all your goods.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: And running is the least effective thing you can do against these guys. Just makes the outcome worse.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Your best option is to hide in the back. Don't let them get to you.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Telara! Whatever happens, stay in the back. Keep yourself hidden.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: The wagon stays silent save for Telara's brief affirmation. Kestrel grips her sword. Her eyes train on the silhouettes as they draw near, through the whirling sands.
Miss Kestrel, I don't think these are your average two-bit highwaymen...
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: If you want to haul your goods out of Sargon with your arms and legs attached, you get back in there and hunker down like I said to.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: They're not gonna lay a finger on you while I'm still alive.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Fine... fine...
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: Heeding the instruction to protect his assets, he promptly retreats back in with a half-croak.
(They're low on numbers... and they're a scattered team. Probably roaming bandits from along the border...)
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: (This is what they came for. Merchants making the crossing...)
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: The fleet-footed bandits press closer without hesitation. Kestrel can see the light of their eyes beneath the shroud of their headscarves.
But their raised machetes glint brighter, caught in the sun's blinding rays.
Who's ready for another round? You guys don't have a hope of touching me!
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: The assailants surrounding the wagon fall back a few steps in spite of themselves, for their opening assault has produced a fresh bandit corpse at Kestrel's feet.
The burdenbeasts lift their hooves in anxiety. Kestrel pulls the reins taut, sword in her right hand. Deep red blood drips from its serrations.
You picked the wrong prey. Run while you can, or else you get this sword through your throats too.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Doesn't matter what's in this wagon, you're not laying a finger on it today.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Kestrel punctuates the declaration by lifting her sword high. Its fresh carmine catches vividly in the vibrant sun, and the few bandits still remaining all shrink away at the sight.
That's right, you should be scared. Let his life serve as a lesson to you.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Make way!
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: The bandits only fall back so far. The wagon is still surrounded. Kestrel can still see the hardness in their eyes. The fear hasn't routed them yet.
(They're not even that strong. I could see myself taking them all out on my own.)
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: (But if these guys are fugitives at the end of their rope...)
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: She reassumes her battle stance, and the bandits facing off against her raise their machetes once more.
Their second rush comes lightning fast, and standing before them, Kestrel recognizes she's not their target.
The wagon!
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: A machete slices at the two burdenbeasts in the front, and the startled animals rear back, whinnying and thrashing madly in an attempt to escape their reins.
The wagon is promptly flung to one side by the bucking beasts. Though Kestrel keeps the bandit's attack from landing, she's helpless to stop the wagon from overturning.
Whargh!
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: The girl tumbles from the wagon clutching her exercise book, and all the loose sheets held between its pages scatter across the arid ground.
Telara! Watch out!
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: While the burdenbeasts kick up dust, the bandits continue pressing in. The bandit leader seizes on his chance, swerves to one side, and charges at Telara.
Guh, dirty asshole!
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Don't even think of touching her!
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: No!
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: Stand up, Telara.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: It's all okay now. They've fled.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Kestrel looks down at the ground by her feet, at a shuddering Telara clutching her hair, as well as the bandit leader whose throat she just skewered.
But behind her, another bandit, sprawled on the ground a moment ago, is now up. While she's turned to check on Telara, the bandit's machete swings for her back.
Kestrel, your back!
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: We need to find something to staunch the wound!
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: It's fine as long as you're safe. I can't let them lay a finger on you.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Gather up all those 'bite marks' you drew. I can walk off a wound like this.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: But these are... look, even if you don't think they're total messes, now there's blood all over them.
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: We should hurry to the market and get your wound looked at instead of worrying about these. They're just scraps... You're still bleeding...
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: Glancing at the bloodstained worksheet in Telara's grasp, Kestrel's only response is to quietly pull the girl to her side, and take the sheet from her hands.
Fresh blood wells forth from the wound on her back, staining the white-painted patterns on her skin red.
Telara, the very first 'bite marks' were painted by warriors in their own blood.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: It was so we would always remember the blood the warriors shed, their sacrifices.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: People started using paint instead long ago, but the significance of it has never changed.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: My 'bite marks' will forever remind me of my responsibility to protect. A non-lethal wound like this will heal before you know it. There's no need to worry.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: But... what about the merchant...
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: I'm alive, I'm alive. Scared me to death, though.
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: The traveling merchant pokes his head out once more from the overturned wagon. His hair is frazzled and his clothes in disarray, but he seems to have escaped any harm.
Thank the heavens all the boxes were strapped down properly. The goods are alright.
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: Miss Kestrel, are you alright? Can we still make it to the border?
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: No problem. The only issue is that the two burdenbeasts got hurt. I know this is the final stretch, but we can't force them.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Someone needs to make a run to the market, and get the wagon back in order.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: I'll go, Kestrel.
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: The bandits backed off for now, but they can't have gone far. No way I'm letting you go alone.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: But... leaving you here isn't safe either.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: How about this, Telara? Take my sword, and get to the market fast.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: They've learned a lesson in blood. They won't try anything against anyone holding the sword that spilled it. It'll protect you. And guarding the merchant will be easy for me back here.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Your sword... okay, no worries, I'll make it to the market safe!
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: Be careful with your wound, Kestrel. Don't overexert yourself.
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: I'll be back as soon as I can!
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: Having an attendant is a real lifesaver, isn't it, Miss Kestrel?
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: She's not an attendant. She's my student.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Or, maybe more like a friend.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Student? But you haven't been teaching her any combat skills. You're one unique warrior, I'll say that much.
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: *sigh* No, never mind. Let's just get the wagon sorted out for now.
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: A unique warrior... you say.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: She picks up Telara's fallen copybook off the ground, carefully places the bloodied 'bite marks' back between its pages, and lets out a long, slow breath.
What a close shave, running into that nonsense right before our destination. Thank goodness I thought of hiring a bodyguard before I left.
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: The wagon needs repairs, and I've had to swap in two fresh burdenbeasts, but at least my wares are all safe.
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: Miss Kestrel, a toast not just to our safe arrival, but in honor of you!
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: Hiring your protection was the best money I've ever spent in my life! Here, cheers!
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: Oh... sure, cheers.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: As long as you two are safe, that's mission complete for me.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: And I guess taking on that gang of desperados is something worth celebrating.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Kestrel toasts, but her gaze is drawn to the side, to Telara silently studying her copybook.
Gulp... pahhh! That hits the spot!
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: Hey, I've been curious, Miss Kestrel. What is it that you and that little apprentice of yours keep fussing over? Drawings?
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: Oh, you mean the 'bite marks'? Yeah, I suppose they're a kind of drawing. Only we Sargonian tribesfolk practice these, no one else does.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Not that many people even know how to draw them these days. I've been meaning to pass the technique down. That's why I took in Telara as a student.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Oh, so barely anyone knows of this practice anymore?
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: Yeah. Every member of our tribe knew how to paint these designs, once upon a time...
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Now... there's a few lucky survivors like me out in the wilds, but they probably don't have anyone to pass it down to.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: I had no idea it was such a rare thing. No wonder you put your life on the line for her.
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: I can see there's real value in these things, just like my wares. Ha-hah!
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: Value? No, that's not the reason I'm fighting for them at all.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Besides, 'bite marks' are priceless, and Telara is especially priceless for agreeing to come with me.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: She abandoned her comfortable life just because she was willing to travel with me...
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: I wouldn't say 'abandoned'. It's not like I wanted to spend my whole life in that town.
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: It was nice enough minding my parents' shop, but you can't just cling to the past forever. That's what I think.
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: So when Kestrel took on a delivery for our place, I asked if she could take me as a student.
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: I once met another group of kids your age. But all I taught them back then was how to fight. It's my fault they never learned what mattered more...
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Kestrel downs her drink in the trailing silence, then stares somewhat distantly into her empty glass.
Knowing what you have to protect... You could say I hadn't fully figured it out back then.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Fascinating, fascinating! This is the first time I've ever heard such a story.
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: The previous bodyguards I've hired were certainly up to snuff, but combat was all most of them knew. I've never met one like you before.
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: What do you mean? There's warriors like me all over the desert. Most people just fail to see a warrior's true side, that's all.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: I say that, but...
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Ah, whatever, let's drink! Telara, put your book down and raise your soda for a toast with us!
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: The escort mission's complete. This is your last chance to say goodbye.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Yes, one more round of drinks! I've still got to make it to the next town before sundown.
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: Empty your glasses, and we'll bid farewell!
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: Took the words out of my mouth! Here!
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Cheers!
attributes.name: All Three

attributes.content: Oof... This Sargonian tipple can really knock you sideways... At least I didn't try to match Miss Kestrel's drinking pace.
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: What an interesting person she was. And their desert artform seemed quite distinctive too, not to mention how rare she says it is...
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: Alright, one final inventory, and then I depart.
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: Box quantity... labels... all fine.
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: Hm? What's this...?
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: One week later, in a market somewhere in Sargon.
Here, this is your cloth. And the red one here is yours. You can pay in a moment, let me tally it up!
attributes.name: Sargonian

attributes.content: Perfect, that's exact change, and then—
attributes.name: Sargonian

attributes.content: Friend! Excuse me, friend!
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: Sorry, can I show you these for a second? Do you know where I can find someone who can draw these?
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: At a draper's stand, a totally foreign merchant makes his excited inquiry as he brandishes a paper sheet filled with patterns.
Anyone here will do. If you find me someone who can draw these, I have a supremely handsome reward for you!
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: Hm? What is this...
attributes.name: Sargonian

attributes.content: The draper gives the designs a puzzled squint, before pulling a disdainful look at once.
Where the hell did you get this from? These are those 'bite marks' the tribesfolk draw on their bodies.
attributes.name: Sargonian

attributes.content: Yes, yes, yes, that's exactly what they're called! So could you find the two people for me who can draw these things?
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: These designs are worth money, you know! I'm willing to pay big, I'll pay buckets, if I can find them and have them draw a few more!
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: Sir, I think you might've been swindled. I can tell at a glance these 'bite marks' are all messed up.
attributes.name: Sargonian

attributes.content: These things are a tribe's heritage. You draw them according to something. What you've got there is wrong—the colors are wrong, the patterns are wrong.
attributes.name: Sargonian

attributes.content: Show anyone else if you don't believe me. No one will tell you the designs on that sheet look alright.
attributes.name: Sargonian

attributes.content: And even if they were fine, they're still just a way for tribal warriors to record their battles. What kind of price are you hoping to fetch with these?
attributes.name: Sargonian

attributes.content: No, no, they're hot commodities in collectors' circles right now. The duo left a legitimate specimen in my wagon, and I had it taken off my hands for a high price in Columbia.
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: Her name was, what was it... yes, Kestrel! Help me find her—
attributes.name: Traveling Merchant

attributes.content: I told you—
attributes.name: Sargonian

attributes.content: Away from the draper's stand, in the bustling market crowd...
Telara, that mark you drew yesterday was beautiful.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Not that anyone's done a design like it before, but compared to all the ones you were trying to copy, it's a huge improvement.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: I feel like you've suddenly gotten it.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Don't you remember, Kestrel? After the merchant left that day, you kept drinking and drinking.
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: We talked about so much that night... not anything to do with 'bite marks', but I remember one thing you said really clearly.
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: You said you were forever a warrior, but you wouldn't be fighting forever.
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: And that got me thinking, maybe 'bite marks' are the same. They can be more than warriors' records of the past.
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: Really... you can look at them as a kind of blessing too. Just like the pattern for protection the chief taught me.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: No, Kestrel, what I mean is, I want to draw everything we see. All of it.
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: Sargon's people, its wilds, and every story we come across. Maybe we can use 'bite marks' to convey all of those.
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: Not in blood, and it doesn't have to be in special colors, or with designs set in stone in the past...
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: Hahaaah!
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: I'm SO glad you came along with me, Telara!
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: I see now... everything the chief did for me. Everything. That's what it all meant.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Meant... what?
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: You know, it's really simple.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: I'm not in a hurry to head back to the branch office. We've got time to do some more wandering.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: I want to see other places like you do. Not just the barrens, but the people and things in places even farther away.
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Let's figure out how to create some new 'bite marks' together!
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Sounds good to me, Kestrel!
attributes.name: Telara

attributes.content: Let's go, then! First up, crossing these barrens!
attributes.name: Kestrel

attributes.content: Kestrel points a finger to the distant horizon, and the two cross the bustling crowd with a grin and make for the wilderness ahead.
In the market crowd under the searing sun, a foreigner waving a piece of paper misses the two of them like cities passing in the night.
